mission.title: oauth2-proxy: Group/Role Access Restriction support in `/oauth2/auth` endpoint
mission.description: Group/Role Access Restriction support in `/oauth2/auth` endpoint. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current oauth2-proxy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your oauth2-proxy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
helm list -n oauth2-proxy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working oauth2-proxy installation.

steps.title: Review oauth2-proxy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant oauth2-proxy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get configmap -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oauth2-proxy
```
Would simplify the deployment of a central Access Proxy for Kubernetes ingresses -- instead of having to support multiple with different OIDC apps that have different RBAC controls managed at that layer.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Group/Role Access Restriction support in `/oauth2/auth`…
steps.description:
Begin to streamline Auth
```yaml
nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/auth-signin: https://access-proxy.example.internal/oauth2/start?rd=https%3A%2F%2F$host$escaped_request_uri
nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/auth-url: https://access-proxy.example.svc.cluster.local/oauth2/auth
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get events -n oauth2-proxy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Group/Role Access Restriction support in `/oauth2/auth`…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/oauth2-proxy/oauth2-proxy/pull/797

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with oauth2-proxy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
